name: Bryansk Oblast (Russia)

colors.symbolism: the historic Soviet banner and the courage of Bryansk partisans during the Great Patriotic War
the gold of the oblast emblem

description: A red field charged at the centre with the gold-coloured coat of arms of Bryansk Oblast.
history: Adopted on 20 December 1998 by the Bryansk Oblast Duma. The red field and overall composition deliberately echo the Soviet-era flag of Bryansk Oblast, in keeping with the region's strong association with World War II partisan resistance.

features.symbolism: the red field consciously echoes the Soviet-era flag of Bryansk Oblast, recalling the partisan resistance in the Bryansk forests during the Great Patriotic War.
a stylised emblem of the oblast featuring a golden fir tree (the Bryansk forest), partisans, sheaves of wheat and an industrial gear; recalls the history and economy of the region.
